claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you looking to claim Child Benefit for?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for my son Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for your son Alex.

Let me ask you: **How old is Alex?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Since Alex is 8 years old, he's under 16, which is within the age range for Child Benefit.

Now I need to ask: **Does Alex live with you in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me and I live in the UK - we're in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for confirming that. 

Just a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** (For example, Alex's other parent or another family member?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

One final question: **Do you have the right to reside in the UK?** For example, are you a UK citizen, or do you have settled status, indefinite leave to remain, or another immigration status that gives you the right to reside here?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- ✓ Alex is 8 years old (under 16)
- ✓ Alex lives with you in the UK
- ✓ You are responsible for Alex
- ✓ You are a UK citizen with the right to reside in the UK
- ✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You meet all the requirements for Child Benefit. You can proceed to make a claim for Alex. If you haven't already claimed, you can apply through HMRC.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, meeting the basic age requirement for Child Benefit. Alex lives with the claimant in the UK (Manchester), which means the claimant is responsible for the child. The claimant is a UK citizen, so they have the right to reside in the UK. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, satisfying the rule that only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen living in Manchester with their 8-year-old son Alex. Alex is under 16 and lives with the claimant, making the claimant responsible for him. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit are satisfied.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, meeting the basic age requirement for Child Benefit. Alex lives with the claimant in the UK (Manchester), which means the claimant is responsible for the child. The claimant is a UK citizen, so they have the right to reside in the UK. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, satisfying the rule that only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen living in Manchester with their 8-year-old son Alex. Alex is under 16 and lives with the claimant, making the claimant responsible for him. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit are satisfied.
